headline: Migrant Worker Health Screening Misses 45% of Communicable Diseases
context: FOMEMA mandatory health screening for foreign workers fails to detect nearly half of communicable disease cases.

cards.big: FOMEMA screening fails to detect 45% of communicable diseases among foreign workers
cards.sub: Single-point screening misses latent TB hepatitis and HIV in window period

cards.big: 2.1 million foreign workers undergo annual FOMEMA screening at RM180 per examination
cards.sub: RM378 million revenue generated annually but screening protocol unchanged since 2008

cards.big: Detected cases result in deportation not treatment creating incentive to avoid screening
cards.sub: Punitive response drives disease underground rather than facilitating public health control

cards.big: Canada screens migrant workers but provides treatment pathways rather than deportation
cards.sub: Treatment-focused approach achieves better disease control outcomes for host population

cards.big: Screening for disease and then deporting rather than treating ensures the disease stays but the patient leaves
Should positive FOMEMA results lead to treatment access rather than automatic deportation
